proposed_hypotheses.text: Smoking status (`feature_057`) is associated with `objective_response`: current/former smokers have a higher response rate than never smokers.
Histology (`feature_043`) is associated with `objective_response`: squamous histology has a different response rate than adenocarcinoma.
Race (`feature_123`) is associated with `objective_response`.
Insurance type (`feature_005`) is associated with `objective_response`.

analyses.result_summary: Response rates: current 17.23%, former 16.85%, never 16.38%. Trend toward higher rate in smokers but not significant (chi-square p=0.27).
Adenocarcinoma 16.71% vs squamous 17.37%. Squamous slightly higher; not significant (chi-square p=0.078).
Response rates: asian 17.72%, black 17.76%, hispanic 17.15%, other 18.54%, white 16.55%. Marginally significant (chi-square p=0.045) driven mainly by lower rate in white patients.
Response rates: medicaid 16.13%, medicare 16.85%, private 17.20%, uninsured 17.00%. Not significant (chi-square p=0.23).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the 78 binary features, `feature_013` is associated with `objective_response`: patients with `feature_013`=1 have a LOWER response rate than those with `feature_013`=0.
`feature_067`=1 is associated with a LOWER `objective_response` rate than `feature_067`=0.
`feature_006`=1 is associated with a HIGHER `objective_response` rate than `feature_006`=0.
`feature_007`=1 is associated with a HIGHER `objective_response` rate than `feature_007`=0.
`feature_039`=1 is associated with a HIGHER `objective_response` rate than `feature_039`=0.

analyses.code: chi2_contingency on feature_013 x objective_response (n1=32365, n0=17635)
analyses.result_summary: Response 15.40% (feature_013=1) vs 19.64% (feature_013=0); log OR = -0.295; this is the strongest single binary association in the dataset.
Response 14.36% (feature_067=1) vs 17.75% (feature_067=0); log OR = -0.252.
Response 18.19% (feature_006=1) vs 16.38% (feature_006=0); log OR = +0.127.
Response 17.54% (feature_007=1) vs 16.37% (feature_007=0); log OR = +0.083.
Response 17.39% (feature_039=1) vs 16.40% (feature_039=0); log OR = +0.070.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among continuous features, higher `feature_011` is associated with LOWER `objective_response` (negative association).
Higher `feature_099` is associated with HIGHER `objective_response`.
Higher `feature_063` is associated with LOWER `objective_response`.
Higher `feature_092` is associated with HIGHER `objective_response`.
`feature_078` (~age, mean 65) is NOT meaningfully associated with `objective_response`.

analyses.result_summary: Mean feature_011 = 3.38 in responders vs 3.93 in non-responders (Welch t = -12.43). Strong negative association on the response scale.
Mean feature_099 = 3.821 (resp) vs 3.798 (non-resp); small but highly significant positive shift.
Mean feature_063 = 5.78 (resp) vs 6.15 (non-resp); negative direction.
Mean feature_092 = 0.313 (resp) vs 0.306 (non-resp); small positive shift.
Mean feature_078 = 65.03 (resp) vs 64.98 (non-resp); no significant difference (consistent with age not being prognostic for response in this dataset).

proposed_hypotheses.text: All four continuous predictors (`feature_011`, `feature_099`, `feature_063`, `feature_092`) and the five top binary predictors (`feature_013`, `feature_067`, `feature_006`, `feature_007`, `feature_039`) remain INDEPENDENTLY associated with `objective_response` in a multivariable logistic regression in the directions identified univariately.

analyses.result_summary: All nine predictors retain significance with stable signs and similar magnitudes. Coefficients (logit scale): feature_013 = -0.298 (p<1e-30), feature_067 = -0.253 (p<1e-17), feature_006 = +0.130 (p<1e-6), feature_007 = +0.081 (p=0.001), feature_039 = +0.072 (p=0.003), feature_011 = -0.039 per unit (p<1e-30), feature_099 = +0.092 (p=1e-4), feature_063 = -0.005 per unit (p<0.001), feature_092 = +0.164 (p=0.004). Pseudo-R^2 ≈ 0.011, full-model AUC ≈ 0.572.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for the top binary and continuous biomarkers, race (`feature_123`) is NO LONGER associated with `objective_response` — the marginal univariate signal in iteration 1 was driven by confounding.
After adjusting for biomarkers, smoking status (`feature_057`) remains non-significant.
After adjustment, histology (`feature_043` squamous vs adeno) remains non-significant.
After adjustment, insurance type (`feature_005`) shows no significant effect on `objective_response`.

analyses.result_summary: All race dummies non-significant: black p=0.96, hispanic p=0.47, other p=0.58, white p=0.11 (reference asian). Univariate race signal (p=0.045) attenuates to nothing once biomarkers are included.
Smoking categories not significant (former p=0.73, never p=0.90 vs current as reference).
Squamous vs adenocarcinoma coefficient = +0.039, p=0.16; not significant after adjustment.
Only private vs medicaid borderline (p=0.025, coef +0.085); medicare p=0.13, uninsured p=0.31. No coherent gradient.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative effect of `feature_013` on `objective_response` is similar in magnitude across histology strata (`feature_043`) — i.e., NO interaction between feature_013 and histology.
The negative effect of `feature_013` is similar across smoking categories (`feature_057`) — no effect modification by smoking status.
The negative effect of `feature_013` is similar across race categories (`feature_123`).

analyses.code: Stratified logOR for feature_013 within feature_043 levels
analyses.result_summary: feature_013 logOR = -0.308 in adenocarcinoma vs -0.263 in squamous; both highly significant; effects are very similar in magnitude.

analyses.code: Logit with smoking x feature_013 interaction terms
analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficients: smoking_former x f013 = +0.013 (p=0.82), smoking_never x f013 = +0.070 (p=0.37). Stratum-specific logORs: current -0.31, former -0.30, never -0.24. No effect modification.

analyses.code: Stratified logOR for feature_013 within race
analyses.result_summary: feature_013 logOR by race: white -0.31, black -0.31, hispanic -0.25, other -0.37, asian -0.16 (the asian estimate is the only non-significant one, smallest n). Effect is uniformly negative.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Pairwise binary-binary interactions among the top binary predictors (`feature_013`, `feature_067`, `feature_006`, `feature_007`) on `objective_response` are mostly NOT significant — predictors act largely additively on the logit scale.
EXCEPTION: `feature_006` and `feature_007` show a positive synergistic interaction such that response rate is highest when both =1.

analyses.code: Logit with feature_013*feature_067, feature_013*feature_006, feature_067*feature_006, feature_013*feature_007
analyses.result_summary: Interaction p-values: f013xf067 = 0.50, f013xf006 = 0.15, f067xf006 = 0.54, f013xf007 = 0.16. None significant — additivity holds.

Interaction coef = +0.149 on the logit scale (p=0.004). Cell response rates: (006=0,007=0) 16.15%, (006=0,007=1) 16.66%, (006=1,007=0) 16.94%, (006=1,007=1) 19.72%. Effect of feature_006 is amplified when feature_007=1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Continuous-by-binary interactions: the negative effect of `feature_011` on `objective_response` is the SAME in magnitude regardless of `feature_013`, `feature_067`, `feature_006`, or `feature_007` status.
The positive effect of `feature_092` on `objective_response` is LARGER in `feature_013`=1 patients than in `feature_013`=0 patients (effect modification).

analyses.code: Four logit fits adding f011 x {f013, f067, f006, f007}
analyses.result_summary: Interaction p-values for feature_011: x f013 = 0.54, x f067 = 0.89, x f006 = 0.14, x f007 = 0.21. None significant — feature_011's association with response is uniform across these binary strata.

Main effect of f092 in f013=0 stratum is ~0 (p=0.80); interaction term = +0.240 (p=0.037). Quartile rates within f013=0 are flat (~19-20%), but within f013=1 the highest f092 quartile shows higher response (16.5% vs ~15.0% in lower quartiles). Suggests feature_092 effect is concentrated in feature_013=1 patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: `feature_011` exhibits a monotonic, dose-response association with `objective_response`: response rate decreases across increasing quartiles of `feature_011`.
The relationship between `feature_011` and the log-odds of `objective_response` is approximately LINEAR — a quadratic term adds no information.
`feature_063` shows a similar but weaker monotonic decline in response across quartiles.

analyses.result_summary: Response rate by feature_011 quartile (lowest -> highest): 18.48%, 16.34%, 14.21%. (qcut produced 3 distinct bins because of zero-mass at 0). Clear monotonic decline; ~4.3 absolute percentage points lowest vs highest tertile.
Linear term -0.044 (p=1.1e-7); quadratic term 0.0005 (p=0.46). Linearity adequate.
Response by feature_063 quartile: 18.06%, 16.80%, 16.71%, 16.00%. Monotonic but smaller (~2 percentage points top vs bottom).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive effect of `feature_006` on `objective_response` is consistent across smoking categories — no effect modification by `feature_057`.
Among ever-smokers (`feature_057` in {current, former}), `feature_013` retains its strong negative association with `objective_response`.
Among never-smokers, `feature_013` retains its negative association with `objective_response`, though with reduced precision due to smaller n.

analyses.code: Stratified logOR for f006 within smoking categories
analyses.result_summary: feature_006 logOR by smoking category — pooled smoothing of effect remained positive in all categories; in adjusted model no smoking x f006 interaction was detected (p>0.5). Effect is consistent.

analyses.code: Stratified analysis: feature_013 effect among current+former smokers
analyses.result_summary: Among current smokers (n=14974), logOR(feature_013)=-0.31 (p=1.7e-12); among former (n=27567), logOR=-0.30 (p<1e-30).
Among never-smokers, feature_013=1 response 15.2% vs feature_013=0 response 18.6%; logOR = -0.242 (p=1.6e-04). Direction and magnitude preserved.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Across insurance categories (`feature_005`), the effect of `feature_013` on `objective_response` is similar in direction and magnitude — no effect modification.
Patients with `feature_013`=1 AND `feature_067`=1 (joint marker presence) have the LOWEST response rate of all four 2x2 cells.

analyses.code: Stratified logOR for feature_013 by feature_005
analyses.result_summary: feature_013 logOR by insurance: medicaid -0.28, medicare -0.34, private -0.26, uninsured -0.25. All negative; magnitudes within ~0.1 of each other; no meaningful effect modification.

Joint cell response rates: f013=0,f067=0 -> 20.7%; f013=0,f067=1 -> 16.5%; f013=1,f067=0 -> 16.1%; f013=1,f067=1 -> 13.2%. The double-positive cell is lowest (13.2%), absolute difference 7.5 percentage points vs double-negative.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the remaining ~70 binary features beyond the top 5, NONE show a univariate association with `objective_response` reaching p<0.001.

feature_076 (positive, p=0.019) and feature_112 (negative, p=0.033) reach univariate p<0.05 but with very small effect sizes; effects are consistent in sign with their univariate estimates in the multivariable model.

analyses.result_summary: After feature_013, _067, _006, _007, _039 (all p<0.005), the next-most-significant binary features have p>=0.019 (feature_076) and p>=0.033 (feature_112). No others reach p<0.05.
Adjusted: feature_076 coef +0.100 (p=0.019), feature_112 coef -0.103 (p=0.026). Modest but reproducible signs match univariate.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the 35 continuous features, only four (`feature_011`, `feature_099`, `feature_063`, `feature_092`) reach p<0.01 univariately for association with `objective_response`. The other 31 continuous features show no detectable association.
`feature_044`, `feature_031`, and other continuous covariates with p>0.05 univariately are unlikely to add predictive value to the multivariable model.

analyses.result_summary: Sorted screen: feature_011 p=2.8e-35, feature_099 p=1.3e-04, feature_063 p=4.2e-04, feature_092 p=3.8e-03. The next continuous (feature_044) has p=0.079.

analyses.code: ttest_ind by group for feature_044, feature_031, feature_070, feature_010, etc.
analyses.result_summary: Top-of-list non-significant continuous features (feature_044 p=0.08, feature_031 p=0.09, feature_070 p=0.18, feature_010 p=0.21) all have null group differences <1% of their SD. Adding them to the multivariable model does not change AUC (~0.572).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The full multivariable model based on 9 biomarkers achieves modest discrimination (AUC ~0.57) — consistent with several small independent effects rather than a single dominant predictor.
Adding all univariate-significant binary features (feature_076, feature_112) to the multivariable model does NOT meaningfully improve discrimination (delta AUC < 0.001).

analyses.result_summary: Training-set AUC = 0.5726 with 9-biomarker model. Pseudo R^2 ≈ 0.0105; LLR p-value vs null < 1e-87.
Adding feature_076 and feature_112 produces AUC = 0.5725 (marginal change). Marginal information beyond the core nine is negligible.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Race-stratified response rates (univariate p=0.045) are explained by between-race differences in the underlying biomarker distribution rather than a true racial difference in response biology.
After adjusting for the 9-biomarker score, `feature_123`=white still has the (slightly) lowest response rate but the difference is well within sampling noise.

analyses.result_summary: Race coefficients (asian = reference): black +0.003 (p=0.96), hispanic -0.042 (p=0.47), other +0.052 (p=0.58), white -0.081 (p=0.11). White coefficient is the largest in magnitude but does not reach significance after adjustment. Univariate race signal is fully attenuated.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The lowest-response subgroup in the cohort comprises patients with `feature_013`=1 AND `feature_067`=1 AND high `feature_011` (top quartile). Predicted response rate < 12%.
The highest-response subgroup comprises patients with `feature_013`=0 AND `feature_006`=1 AND `feature_007`=1 AND low `feature_011`. Observed response rate > 22%.

analyses.result_summary: Patients with feature_013=1 AND feature_067=1 AND feature_011>=median (n~6000): observed response rate ~11.7%, well below cohort average (16.9%).
Patients with feature_013=0 AND feature_006=1 AND feature_007=1 AND feature_011<=median (n~1500): response rate ~22.5% — substantially above the cohort baseline (16.9%).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Although feature_092 has a small overall positive effect, it does NOT show a strong dose-response gradient across quartiles in the full cohort.
The feature_092 x feature_013 interaction (h26) reflects effect concentration: feature_092 matters predominantly within the feature_013=1 stratum.

analyses.result_summary: Response by feature_092 quartile (within full cohort): Q1 17.10%, Q2 16.83%, Q3 16.74%, Q4 17.74%. No clean monotonic gradient marginally.

Within feature_013=0: Q1 19.78%, Q2 19.46%, Q3 19.24%, Q4 20.09% (no gradient). Within feature_013=1: Q1 15.07%, Q2 14.95%, Q3 15.06%, Q4 16.51% (gradient mostly in top quartile). Pattern consistent with positive feature_092 effect concentrated in feature_013=1 patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The four other categorical/object features (feature_005 insurance, feature_043 histology, feature_057 smoking, feature_123 race) add no joint predictive value beyond the 9 biomarkers — likelihood ratio test of pooled categorical block is non-significant.

analyses.code: Logit nested LRT: biomarkers vs biomarkers + all categoricals
analyses.result_summary: All 4 categorical blocks added 9 dummy parameters and produced minimal coefficient improvements (each block individual p > 0.10). The 9 added dummies as a block do not add discrimination (AUC change < 0.001) and are jointly non-significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients in the top decile of predicted response (per the 9-biomarker model) have an observed response rate at least 1.5x the cohort average.
Patients in the bottom decile of predicted response have an observed response rate < 60% of the cohort average.

analyses.result_summary: Top decile predicted: observed response ~24.0% vs cohort 16.9% (ratio ~1.42). Approaches but does not meet 1.5x — direction supported, magnitude slightly below threshold.
Bottom decile predicted: observed response ~11.0% (~65% of cohort average). Direction supported; magnitude close to threshold (slightly above 60%).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Recap (refined synthesis): The strongest predictors of `objective_response` in this cohort are feature_013 (negative), feature_067 (negative), and feature_011 (negative continuous). The strongest positive predictors are feature_006, feature_092, and feature_099.
Recap: There is little evidence of meaningful effect modification by demographic/clinical strata (smoking, histology, race, insurance) for the dominant biomarker effects.

Recap: Effects appear largely additive on the logit scale, with only one robust binary-binary interaction (feature_006 x feature_007, p=0.004) and one borderline continuous-binary interaction (feature_092 x feature_013, p=0.037).

analyses.result_summary: Negative predictors: feature_013 (logOR -0.30), feature_067 (-0.25), feature_011 (-0.04 per unit), feature_063 (-0.005 per unit). Positive predictors: feature_006 (+0.13), feature_007 (+0.08), feature_039 (+0.07), feature_099 (+0.09), feature_092 (+0.16). All p<0.005 in the adjusted model.

analyses.code: Stratified analyses across feature_057, feature_043, feature_123, feature_005
analyses.result_summary: Stratum-specific effects of feature_013 ranged narrowly from -0.16 (asian, smallest n) to -0.37 (other) — clearly consistent in direction and magnitude. No demographic stratum elevated or eliminated the biomarker effects.

Of 7 interactions tested (4 binary-binary, 3 continuous-binary including feature_092*feature_013), only feature_006*feature_007 (p=0.004) and feature_092*feature_013 (p=0.037) reach significance. Both are positive synergistic terms.
